steps.title: Spot the term needing definition
steps.content: The previous sentence introduces the term "annular" without explaining it. The blank sentence defines what annular means: the Moon didn't cover the Sun completely.

steps.title: Select the clarification transition
steps.content:
The logical relationship is specification (definition). The blank needs a transition signaling that the next sentence restates or defines the word "annular."
  • Choice A — "That is,": signals a restatement or definition of a term just introduced, fitting the explanation of "annular," so fits. ✓
  • Choice B — "Nonetheless,": signals continuation despite an obstacle—no obstacle is present here, so fails. ✗
  • Choice C — "Meanwhile,": signals simultaneous events happening at the same time, so fails. ✗
  • Choice D — "For example,": signals a specific instance of a general claim; the blank sentence defines "annular," it doesn't give an example of a general rule, so fails. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
